pending.body:
finding_summary: A `run sync-milestone` replay can permanently stamp a failed attempt with the synthetic `cli-sync:<project_id>` task id, and later daemon replays never repair it back to the real failed task id.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/cli/run.rs:1321-1339
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:211-226
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:571-588
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:728-780
remediation_hint: Treat `cli-sync:` task ids as weak provenance. If a later replay for the same `(bead_id, project_id, run_id, started_at)` arrives with a non-synthetic task id, repair the stored `task_id` and structured `outcome_detail`/journal details instead of preserving the placeholder forever.

finding_summary: Stale failure replays that discover a newer retry only backfill the `BeadFailed` journal event and never repair the missing historical `TaskRunEntry`, so retry history remains permanently incomplete in task-run lineage.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:229-245
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:283-306
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2418-2476
remediation_hint: In the stale-replay branches, repair or append the exact historical failed attempt in task-run lineage as well as the journal event, while still skipping controller mutation when a newer retry exists.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 17 -> Plan and Implement
